rssd.name: RSSD ID
rssd.issuer: Federal Reserve Board (National Information Center / RSSD database)
rssd.format: Sequential integer with check digit; no fixed length; carries no inherent attribute information
rssd.scope: Every entity the Fed tracks: banks, BHCs/FHCs, branches, savings institutions, holding-company tiers, and some foreign and non-bank entities
rssd.reuse_rule: Never changes, never reused; one per entity for its entire life cycle; persists after liquidation or closure
primary_in: FR Y-9C and the FR Y-series
rssd.notes: The canonical crosswalk spine and most stable join key across charter changes and over time. Field name ID_RSSD in NIC. Also carried by FDIC datasets (FED_RSSD), FFIEC CDR, FRED bank series, academic panels, and HMDA pre-2018 (agency code 2).

fdic_cert.name: FDIC Certificate number
fdic_cert.scope: Every FDIC-insured depository institution (banks and savings institutions); assigned per head-office depository
fdic_cert.reuse_rule: Unique per insured institution; assigned at insurance and retained through life; not recycled in practice
fdic_cert.notes: Sequential-integer system: not globally unique without an agency qualifier. Field name ID_FDIC_CERT in NIC. Also appears as a HMDA pre-2018 respondent id (agency code 3); FDIC records carry RSSD as FED_RSSD.

occ_charter.name: OCC Charter number
occ_charter.issuer: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
occ_charter.scope: National banks and (since 2011-07-21) federal savings associations / federal thrifts
occ_charter.reuse_rule: Assigned at chartering and retained through the institution's life
occ_charter.notes: Since 2011-07-21 the OCC Charter field also stores national thrifts as thrift docket + 700000. Field name ID_OCC in NIC. Also appears as a HMDA pre-2018 respondent id (agency code 1).

ncua.name: NCUA Charter / ID number
ncua.format: Sequential integer; banded 1-59999 Federal, 60000-79999 Federally Insured State Chartered, 80000+ Non-Federally Insured
ncua.scope: Federally insured credit unions (and some non-federally-insured)
ncua.reuse_rule: Assigned at chartering and retained through life
ncua.notes: Sequential-integer system: not globally unique without an agency qualifier. Field name ID_NCUA in NIC. Also appears as a HMDA pre-2018 respondent id (agency code 5).

lei.name: LEI (Legal Entity Identifier, ISO 17442)
lei.issuer: GLEIF governs; codes issued by accredited Local Operating Units (LOUs) overseen by the LEI Regulatory Oversight Committee (ROC)
lei.format: 20-char alphanumeric: chars 1-4 LOU prefix, chars 5-18 entity-unique string, chars 19-20 MOD-97-10 checksum (ISO/IEC 7064)
lei.scope: Any legal entity participating in financial transactions, globally
lei.reuse_rule: Permanently unique to one entity; never reused; must be renewed annually to stay active but the code itself persists
lei.notes: Exists only from ~2012 forward and only for entities that obtained one; cannot back-fill pre-2012 panels (coverage gap). Field name ID_LEI in NIC. Also used in SEC/CFTC swap reporting, FR Y-series LEI fields, and the GLEIF golden-copy file.

aba.name: ABA Routing Transit Number (RTN)
aba.issuer: American Bankers Association via its registrar (currently LexisNexis Risk Solutions; historically Accuity / Thomson Financial)
aba.format: 9 digits FFFF AAAA C: Fed routing symbol (4) + ABA institution id (4) + MOD-10 check digit (1)
aba.scope: Financial institutions eligible for a Federal Reserve master account; identifies a payment-routing account, not the legal entity
aba.reuse_rule: Tied to payment routing not entity identity; reassignable/retirable after mergers; reusable across non-concurrent entities; one institution may hold many
aba.notes: NOT an entity key - never use an RTN as a persistent entity identifier. Field name ID_ABA_PRIM in NIC (no two concurrent entities share one).

ots.name: OTS Docket number / Thrift ID
ots.issuer: (former) Office of Thrift Supervision - defunct since 2011-07-21
ots.format: Numeric docket; NIC field is 9 chars but only the right-most 5 are used
ots.reuse_rule: Legacy identifier; OTS functions transferred to OCC/FDIC/Fed under Dodd-Frank; new thrift supervision uses OCC Charter / RSSD
ots.notes: Field names ID_THRIFT and ID_THRIFT_HC (OTS-assigned 'H'+5-digit holding-company id) in NIC. Also a HMDA pre-2018 respondent id (agency code 4, legacy).

ein.name: Federal Tax ID / EIN
ein.scope: Any US entity with tax obligations
ein.reuse_rule: Permanently assigned to a legal entity; not recycled to another entity
ein.notes: Not a primary bank-regulatory key. Multiple entities under one parent each have their own EIN. Field name ID_TAX in NIC (effective 2008-12-31). Also appears in SEC filings and as a HMDA pre-2018 fallback respondent id.

cik.name: CIK (Central Index Key)
cik.format: Integer, zero-padded to 10 digits in API paths (CIK##########)
cik.scope: Any EDGAR filer: companies (including public BHCs), funds, and individuals
cik.reuse_rule: Assigned at filer signup; unique to the filer and not recycled
cik.notes: Independent of RSSD/CERT; joining public-BHC financials to regulatory data requires an external crosswalk (commonly CIK -> cert -> RSSD). UNVERIFIED that any official SEC<->Fed CIK/RSSD mapping is published.

cusip.name: CUSIP
cusip.issuer: CUSIP Global Services (operated for the American Bankers Association)
cusip.scope: Securities (equity/debt) of issuers, including public BHCs
cusip.reuse_rule: Identifies a security, not the legal entity; reassignment governed by CGS rules
cusip.notes: Field name ID_CUSIP in NIC; a searchable field in SEC EDGAR full-text search. Related: ticker symbols (issued by the listing exchange NYSE/Nasdaq, 1-5 alpha chars) can change or be reused across issuers and are likewise not a stable entity key.
